# Premium Proposal — Prior Deals at this Customer (Anonymised Summary)

## Purpose

Summary of recent indicative and firm offers made to this customer, captured for pattern recognition and reuse on new opportunities.

---

## Deal A — MCN expansion, UAE (~$45M)

- **Year:** 2023
- **Outcome:** Won
- **Scope:** Radio capacity uplift across approximately 600 macro sites, including massive MIMO on dense urban subset
- **Commercial construct:** Multi-year framework agreement with volume rebate tiers tied to deployment milestones. List price held within standard regional reference range
- **Software attach:** Standard radio software release plus basic optimisation features. Customer declined AI-RAN package at this stage
- **Services:** Full integration and project management; customer retained operations
- **Pricing structure:** Standard regional pricing, structured incentive achieved low single-digit effective discount via multi-year commitment
- **Key terms:** Standard liability cap structure, 3PP cost pass-through within band, indexation capped at 4%
- **Lessons captured:** Customer accepted construct-led commercial movement when given a credible multi-year framework. AI-RAN package was rejected as "future scope"; should have been positioned as foundational rather than premium

---

## Deal B — Private network for enterprise vertical (~$22M)

- **Year:** 2024
- **Outcome:** Lost (to Nokia)
- **Scope:** Dedicated private 5G network for major enterprise customer of the operator
- **Commercial construct:** Single-vendor proposal, three-year managed-services component
- **Pricing structure:** Aggressive ask from customer (12% below regional reference). Ericsson held within standard regional reference. Customer closed with Nokia at a reported lower price point
- **Key terms:** Not finalised
- **Lessons captured:** Customer used Nokia anchor aggressively in this segment. Internal post-mortem concluded the loss was acceptable — matching Nokia would have contaminated framework reference across the wider radio relationship for short-term revenue. Confirmed the value of holding the line on regional reference; the wider account relationship was not damaged

---

## Deal C — Transport network upgrade (~$18M)

- **Year:** 2024
- **Outcome:** Won
- **Scope:** Backhaul transport upgrade across UAE footprint
- **Commercial construct:** Standard framework pricing, one-time deal structure
- **Software attach:** Network management and orchestration package included
- **Services:** Standard install and integration
- **Lessons captured:** Bundled NMS software-attach included at signing was a meaningful differentiator vs. competitor proposals. Customer's CFO function flagged total package economics over pure transport pricing

---

## Deal D — Mid-band capacity uplift, multi-OPCO (~$70M)

- **Year:** 2025
- **Outcome:** Won
- **Scope:** Cross-OPCO mid-band capacity uplift across three OPCOs in the group
- **Commercial construct:** Group framework agreement with cross-OPCO volume aggregation. Software-attach incentive structure tied to AI-RAN deployment milestones across the OPCOs
- **Pricing structure:** Group-level pricing with construct-led commercial movement. List price held at regional reference; effective benefit through aggregation and software-attach incentive
- **Key terms:** Standard liability and IP indemnity structures held. Indexation cap pushed to 3.5% by customer, held by Ericsson. 3PP cost pass-through within ±10% band agreed
- **Lessons captured:** Cross-OPCO aggregation was a meaningful structural lever. Customer's CFO function engaged early; deal shape was negotiated at group level before OPCO-level commercial detail. Confirmed the AI-RAN attach as a viable commercial lever at this customer

---

## Patterns observable across these deals

- **Customer responds well to construct-led movement.** All three won deals closed on structured construct (multi-year, software attach, cross-OPCO aggregation), not on list-price match.
- **List-price holds at this customer.** No major deal in the period closed on list-price reduction below regional reference. The one deal where matching would have been required (Deal B) was lost without damaging the wider relationship.
- **CFO function engagement is decisive on large deals.** Deals where the CFO function engaged early closed cleaner. Deals where the CFO function reviewed only late (Deal B) closed harder or were lost.
- **Software attach is a credible lever.** Has worked when positioned as foundational (Deals C and D), failed when positioned as premium (Deal A).
- **Indexation is the consistent fight.** Customer pushes for sub-3% indexation cap every time. Ericsson has consistently held at 3.5-4%.

---

*Note: All figures and outcomes are illustrative for MVP demonstration purposes only.*
